name: Star of Bethlehem (Coffeecake)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 stick Unsalted Butter, melted and cooled
recipeInstructions:
For Dough:
1. Sprinkle yeast and a pinch of sugar over warm water in a large bowl; stir to dissolve.
2. Let stand until foamy, about 5 minutes.
3. Mix in butter, eggs, salt, and remaining sugar.
4. Mix in enough flour (1 cup at a time) to form stiff dough.
5. Cover and chill at least 2 hours. It can be prepared up to 1 day ahead.
For Filling:
1. Stir first 4 ingredients in a heavy small saucepan over low heat until sugar dissolves.
2. Cover and simmer until mixture thickens and apricots soften, about 10 minutes.
3. Butter two 12-inch diameter pizza pans.
4. Divide dough into 4 pieces. Roll out 1 piece on lightly floured surface into an 8-inch round.
5. Transfer to one prepared pan. Spread half of the filling over, leaving a 1/2-inch border at the edge.
6. Sprinkle with 1/4 cup pecans.
7. Roll second dough piece into an 8-inch round. Place atop filled round and press edges to seal.
8. Using lightly floured 2-inch diameter cookie cutter, cut through the center of the dough; do not remove the dough round.
9. Using lightly floured pastry wheel, starting 1/4 inch from the edge of the inner round, cut all the way through the dough to the outside edge.
10. Repeat 11 times, spacing cuts 1 1/2 inches apart at the outer edge.
11. Pick up the end of one dough strip, twist once, and replace on the baking sheet. Repeat with remaining dough strips, forming a star shape.
12. Repeat rolling, filling, cutting, and twisting with the remaining dough and filling.
13. Let dough rise in a warm area until almost doubled, about 35 minutes.
14. Preheat oven to 375F.
15. Brush glaze over breads. Sprinkle 1/4 cup pecans over each.
16. Bake until tops are golden brown, about 15 minutes. Transfer to racks.
For Icing:
1. Place sugar in a small bowl.
2. Mix in enough cream (by teaspoonfuls) to form pourable icing.
3. Mix in vanilla.
4. Drizzle icing over warm coffee cakes.
5. Serve warm or at room temperature.
